content:
Laser cleaning has emerged as a highly effective, non-abrasive method for removing contaminants from {material} substrates in industries like aerospace and automotive. Residual oxides or coatings, often measured via surface roughness (Ra in µm) or contaminant mass removal (mg/cm²), can be challenging. Contrary to abrasive blasting assumptions, laser cleaning minimizes substrate damage while achieving near-complete residue removal. For {material}, effectiveness varies with laser parameters like pulse energy (mJ) and wavelength (nm). Below is a JSON chart dataset reflecting cleaning efficiency metrics on {material} surfaces, noting slight variances due to substrate porosity or oxide thickness.
